mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate earnings prospects weaken.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5% for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days as higher discount rates reduce equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in >60% of instances where CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS both rise by >0.5% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1600 thousand units signals weak demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days as construction-related sectors underperform.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in >60% of instances where HOUST remains below 1600 for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 112.0 signals strong supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as manufacturing sectors outperform.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in >60% of instances where INDPRO exceeds 112.0 for a month.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence between rising payrolls (PAYEMS) and stable unemployment (UNRATE) indicates labor market strength, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days as corporate hiring boosts earnings expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in >60% of instances where PAYEMS rises >100k and UNRATE remains stable.

mechanism_narrative: A 4-quarter decline in GDP (GDPC1) below 11500 billion dollars signals economic contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days as recession risks materialize.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in >60% of instances where GDPC1 remains below 11500 for 4 consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month rise in the federal funds rate above 5.0% signals aggressive monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as borrowing costs weigh on equities.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in >60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0% for a month.
